title: What Causes the Northern Lights
script_summary: Explore the science behind the breathtaking Northern Lights.

segments.voiceover: The aurora is solar wind slamming into Earth's magnetic shield — and making the sky glow.
But what is solar wind? It's a stream of charged particles from the sun, racing through space.
When solar wind hits our magnetosphere, it creates a stunning light show — the Northern Lights.
NASA explains that these particles excite gases in our atmosphere, causing vibrant colors.
So, next time you see the auroras, you'll know: it's the sun's energy lighting up our sky!
Like and follow for more mind-blowing science discoveries!
